groq.notes: OpenAI-compatible Groq API. Model list should be probed before a full run.
groq.quota_notes: Use model-specific conservative RPM because Groq preview/production limits vary by model; all selected models remain below 320 RPD/model for this benchmark.

cerebras.notes: OpenAI-compatible Cerebras Inference API. Conservative per-model RPM for free-tier runs.
cerebras.quota_notes: Free-tier planning assumption: 5 RPM/model, 30K TPM/model, 1M TPD/model; run at 4 RPM/model.

mistral.notes: Mistral chat completions API. Current limits should be checked in the console/headers.
mistral.quota_notes: Use model-specific conservative RPM/RPS. Combined Mistral schedule is kept near or below 1 RPS to reduce free-tier 429 risk.

google.quota_notes: Supplementary provider. OpenAI-compatible Gemini endpoint. Run 20-prompt probe before scaling.

nvidia.quota_notes: Supplementary provider via NVIDIA Integrate OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Probe 20 prompts first; do not hard-fail core benchmark.

xai.notes: OpenAI-compatible xAI API. Official tier-0 limits list 30 RPS and 10M TPM per model for grok-4.3/grok-build-0.1; run probe before full collection.
xai.quota_notes: Use conservative 40 RPM despite official RPS being much higher to validate account/model behavior and avoid burst effects.

fireworks.notes: OpenAI-compatible Fireworks serverless API. Strong open-model catalog; adaptive TPM limits. Probe first.
fireworks.quota_notes: Starting serverless account limits are token-based/adaptive; 40 RPM is conservative for short ClinMAP prompts.

deepinfra.notes: OpenAI-compatible DeepInfra API. Official default limit is concurrent-request based, not RPM; probe first.
deepinfra.quota_notes: 40 RPM is well below default concurrency-based throughput if the account has credits and the model is healthy.

together.notes: OpenAI-compatible Together serverless API. Dynamic per-model rate limits improve with steady traffic.
together.quota_notes: Ramp gradually; no fixed published per-model RPM on serverless.

models.notes: Added as a widely recognized NVIDIA-hosted Meta Llama baseline. Not a headline frontier model; useful for comparison against newer Nemotron/GLM/DeepSeek hosted models.

supplementary_provider_policy.default_behavior: candidate config only; run explicit models/providers after key-enabled probe
supplementary_provider_policy.provenance_rule: Every new hosted source has its own provider and model_alias; do not relabel existing NVIDIA rows as direct-provider rows.
supplementary_provider_policy.scale_rule: Probe model availability, then run --limit 20 at target RPM, then full --retry-failed/new run-id only if 429/5xx rate is acceptable.
supplementary_provider_policy.openrouter_note: OpenRouter omitted from this runner config because provider routing fields are not currently recorded/requested; use only with a runner patch that records routing/provider metadata or label rows as openrouter-routed.
